# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create Kafka broker configuration file (server.properties) for production deployment. Configure listeners, log directories, and replication settings. Set up Zookeeper connection and broker ID. This is for our distributed Kafka cluster.
# Category: kafka_topic_authorization

# server.properties

broker.id=1 # Replace with your desired Broker ID
host.name=localhost # replace localhost if its different 
port=9092 # Listening port for communication between broker and clients
log.dirs=/tmp/kafka-logs # Log directory where logs are stored
num.network.threads=3 # Number of threads in the network process, which handles sending and receiving messages across all the brokers
num.io.threads=8 #Number of threads for handling I/O operations such as log flushing to disk or producing to topics that have replication factors greater than 1
socket.send.buffer.bytes=102400 # Buffer size in bytes for TCP send buffer, increase if you expect high traffic. The OS may also limit this value. This sets the socket buffer size for data sent to clients
socket.receive.buffer.bytes=10240<｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>8567 # Buffer size in bytes for TCP receive buffer, increase if you expect high traffic. This is effectively how large a single request from any client can be
num.partitions=1 # Number of partitions per topic. More partitions allow higher concurrency and provide better throughput over time
log.retention.minutes=10080 # Time to retain log data, default 7 days (10080 minutes)
log.roll.hours=168 # Maximum hours to retain a single partition's worth of log data after which old data is deleted and a new file is created
log.segment.bytes=10485760 # Log segment size in bytes before deleting old log data, default 1GB (10MB * 1024)
num.recovery.threads.per.data.dir=2 # Number of recovery threads per data directory to be used for log recovery. The setting controls how the brokers are load balanced when doing partition reassignment and log compaction in the case where we have a large number of partitions
log.cleaner.enable=default # Whether or not to enable cleaner threads, defaults to true
num.replicas=1 #Number of copies of data kept on different nodes for fault tolerance, 1 means one copy on one node and no redundancy. If it is more than that then multiple nodes are involved in data replication.
offsets.topic.replication.factor=1
group.initial.rebalance.ms=60000 # Max time to wait before considering a consumer dead when subscribing for the first time
auto.create.topics.enable=true # Automatically create topics that don't exist, defaults to true but set it to false if you want manual topic creation control
delete.topic.enable=true # Allow delete operations on existing topics, default value is false. It’s typically not recommended as deletion of data in Kafka could lead to loss of valuable data. So it should be kept false under production environment.
